what: Remove CUDA graphs and Q quantization caching to fix LB correctness. Keep fp8-all-shapes and non-persistent/persistent split logic.
why: CUDA graphs replay stale computation when LB changes input data each iteration (recheck=True). Q fp8 cache also returns stale results for new data at same pointer. All kv=8192 shapes fail with thousands of mismatches.

what: Call mla_decode_stage1_asm_fwd directly for all shapes, pre-allocate all buffers, skip reduce for splits=1
why: mla_decode_fwd creates temporary tensors each call (logits, attn_lse, final_lse). Direct asm call with pre-allocated buffers avoids per-call allocations. For splits=1, output is written directly, skipping reduce.

what: Use bf16 Q with fp8 KV (a16w8 kernel) to skip Q quantization overhead
why: Q quantization (per_tensor_quant_hip) costs ~5-10us per call. The a16w8 MLA kernel exists in aiter (mla_a16w8_qh16_m16x4_n16x1_coex0_mask1_ps.co). Using bf16 Q directly with fp8 KV saves quant cost while keeping fp8 KV bandwidth savings.
